content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 13, 1988


Honorable Kay Wilson
City Councilmember
City of Santa Paula
123 North Tenth St.
Santa Paula, CA 93060

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-88-420

Dear Councilmember Wilson:

This is in response to your request for advice regarding
your responsibilities under the conflict-of-interest provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/ Because your
question relates to past conduct, we are unable to provide you
with advice regarding your participation in the decisions in
question. (Regulation 18329(b)(8((A), copy enclosed.)

Should you be faced with a decision affecting the same or
similar interests in the future, we would be happy to provide
you with advice regarding your responsibilities under the Act
at that time.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths/
General Counsel

By: Lilly Spitz
Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:LS:plh

Enclosure

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code
of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code
of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

FPP
Kay Wilson
Oct 31 123 North Tenth St.
10 54 Santa Paula, CA 93060
00


October 25, 1988


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Attention: Lilly Spitz

It has been brought to my attention that I may have erred in voting on
a development on the hillsides of Santa Paula at last night's Council
meeting. The development passed 4-0 with one abstention by a Council
member whose home is adjacent to this project at 713 Loma Vista.

The project is for 69 homes on 50+ acres. The developer asked for 73
homes along with a request for variance which was denied.

In May of 1988, one of my real estate agents listed for sale a home
immediately adjacent to this bareland for $450,000. The owners had
not yet found a home of their choice and were testing the market for
price. On June 20, 1988, after finding that home in Ventura, they
reduced the price to $395,000, which was our original estimate of
value. On September 3, 1988, the owners received an offer for
$395,000 which they accepted and that escrow is scheduled to close on
December 2, 1988.

Full disclosure was made to the buyers relative to the proposed
project which they felt would do two things: decrease the element of
privacy in this exclusive area, but at the same time would upgrade the
area; the result being no net effect on value.

It is my understanding that the fee appraisal indicated no net effect
on the value by the proposed project. Our real estate sign was taken
down and the home is now vacant.

I did participate in the discussion by the Council; however, my own
determination of "no conflict" may be in error. Will you please
review these facts and assist me in this matter. If I have erred,
please advise on what corrective action I must take at this point.

If you will remember, I discussed with you a few weeks ago whether or
not I had a conflict with voting on the City's Rent Control Ordinance
in view of the fact that my company has listings in mobile home parks.

Fair Political Practices Commission
Page 2


You indicated in that conversation that I would have a conflict only
if the value of the coach was affected by the ordinance. I view this
current situation to the similar in that the value of this house on
Loma Vista was not affected by the proposed development.

Please call me at (805) 525-5523.  Obviously, I need to move quickly
on this matter.

Sincerely yours,

Kay Wilson

Kay Wilson
Council Member
City of Santa Paula

cc:  Phil Romney, City Attorney
     Bob Livingston, City Administrator
     City Council
     City Clerk

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 1, 1988


Kay Wilson
123 North Tenth Street
Santa Paula, CA 93060

Re: 88-420

Dear Ms. Wilson:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on October 31, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact Lilly Spitz, an attorney in the
Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to information needed. If your request is for
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can.
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec.
18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[illegible] & [illegible]
Diane M. Griffiths for
General Counsel

DMG:plh

Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
